title: Flutter app architecture design
prompt: Design the mobile architecture for our fintech app using Flutter
expected_behavior: Produces all 6 sections with Flutter-specific guidance: Impeller renderer, BLoC/Riverpod state management, offline-first with drift/sqflite, performance targets (<2s cold start), BFF pattern, and CI/CD with Fastlane + Shorebird OTA.

title: Platform selection decision
prompt: Should we go native or cross-platform for our new consumer app? We have a JavaScript team.
expected_behavior: Presents the S1 platform comparison matrix, identifies React Native New Architecture as strong fit given JS team, discusses trade-offs (Fabric/TurboModules benefits), and provides clear decision criteria.

title: Offline-first for field workers
prompt: Our field workers need an app that works without internet in remote areas
expected_behavior: Emphasizes S3 offline-first design: persistent action queue, conflict resolution strategy (CRDT or field-level merge), background sync with WorkManager/BGTaskScheduler, and optimistic UI patterns.

title: Super app with multiple feature teams
prompt: We're building a super app with 8 feature teams, each owning a module
expected_behavior: Covers micro-frontend architecture, dynamic feature delivery (Play Feature Delivery / On-Demand Resources), navigation contracts between modules, feature module independence enforcement.

title: False positive — existing app health check
prompt: Our app has a 2% crash rate and slow cold starts, can you assess what's wrong?
expected_behavior: Does NOT activate this skill. Recognizes this is an assessment of an existing app (AS-IS), not new architecture design. Defers to mobile-assessment skill.
